steps.title: Translate the English inequality into algebraic form
steps.content:
The phrase x is at most 2 less than 3 times the value of y breaks down as:
  • at most means $\leq$
  • 2 less than 3 times the value of y means $3y - 2$
Combining these: $$x \leq 3y - 2$$

steps.title: Substitute y = −4 and find the maximum value of x
steps.content:
Plugging in $y = -4$ into the inequality:
$$x \leq 3(-4) - 2$$
$$x \leq -12 - 2$$
$$x \leq -14$$
Since $x$ can be at most $-14$, the greatest possible value of $x$ is $-14$.
